baseModel.name: ProxyResource
baseModel.doc: The resource model definition for a Azure Resource Manager proxy resource. It will not have tags and a location

baseModel.name: Resource
baseModel.doc: Common fields that are returned in the response for all Azure Resource Manager resources

properties.name: id
properties.doc: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"

properties.name: systemData
properties.doc: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

type.name: SystemData
type.doc: Metadata pertaining to creation and last modification of the resource.

properties.name: createdBy
properties.doc: The identity that created the resource.

properties.name: createdByType
properties.doc: The type of identity that created the resource.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource creation (UTC).

properties.name: lastModifiedBy
properties.doc: The identity that last modified the resource.

properties.name: lastModifiedByType
properties.doc: The type of identity that last modified the resource.

properties.name: lastModifiedAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource last modification (UTC)

properties.name: location
properties.doc: Azure Resource Manager geo location of the resource.

properties.name: properties
properties.doc: Properties of availability state.

properties.name: availabilityState
properties.doc: Availability status of the resource. When it is null, this availabilityStatus object represents an availability impacting event

properties.name: title
properties.doc: Title description of the availability status.

properties.name: summary
properties.doc: Summary description of the availability status.

properties.name: detailedStatus
properties.doc: Details of the availability status.

properties.name: reasonType
properties.doc: When the resource's availabilityState is Unavailable, it describes where the health impacting event was originated. Examples are planned, unplanned, user initiated or an outage etc.

properties.name: context
properties.doc: When an event is created, it can either be triggered by a customer or the platform of the resource and this field will illustrate that. This field is connected to the category field in this object.

properties.name: category
properties.doc: When a context field is set to Platform, this field will reflect if the event was planned or unplanned. If the context field does not have a value of Platform, then this field will be ignored.

properties.name: articleId
properties.doc: The Article Id

properties.name: rootCauseAttributionTime
properties.doc: When the resource's availabilityState is Unavailable, it provides the Timestamp for when the health impacting event was received.

properties.name: healthEventType
properties.doc: In case of an availability impacting event, it describes when the health impacting event was originated. Examples are Lifecycle, Downtime, Fault Analysis etc.

properties.name: healthEventCause
properties.doc: In case of an availability impacting event, it describes where the health impacting event was originated. Examples are PlatformInitiated, UserInitiated etc.

properties.name: healthEventCategory
properties.doc: In case of an availability impacting event, it describes the category of a PlatformInitiated health impacting event. Examples are Planned, Unplanned etc.

properties.name: healthEventId
properties.doc: It is a unique Id that identifies the event

properties.name: ResolutionEta
properties.doc: When the resource's availabilityState is Unavailable and the reasonType is not User Initiated, it provides the date and time for when the issue is expected to be resolved.

properties.name: occuredTime
properties.doc: Timestamp for when last change in health status occurred.

properties.name: reasonChronicity
properties.doc: Chronicity of the availability transition.

properties.name: reportedTime
properties.doc: Timestamp for when the health was last checked.

properties.name: recentlyResolved
properties.doc: An annotation describing a change in the availabilityState to Available from Unavailable with a reasonType of type Unplanned

type.name: ResourceHealthAvailabilityStateRecentlyResolved
type.doc: An annotation describing a change in the availabilityState to Available from Unavailable with a reasonType of type Unplanned

properties.name: unavailableOccuredTime
properties.doc: Timestamp for when the availabilityState changed to Unavailable

properties.name: resolvedTime
properties.doc: Timestamp when the availabilityState changes to Available.

properties.name: unavailableSummary
properties.doc: Brief description of cause of the resource becoming unavailable.

properties.name: recommendedActions
properties.doc: Lists actions the user can take based on the current availabilityState of the resource.

valueType.name: ResourceHealthRecommendedAction
valueType.doc: Lists actions the user can take based on the current availabilityState of the resource.

properties.name: action
properties.doc: Recommended action.

properties.doc: Link to the action

properties.name: ActionUriComment
properties.doc: the comment for the Action

properties.name: ActionUriText
properties.doc: Substring of action, it describes which text should host the action URL.

properties.doc: Lists the service impacting events that may be affecting the health of the resource.

valueType.doc: Lists the service impacting events that may be affecting the health of the resource.

properties.name: eventTags
properties.doc:
A list of metadata tags associated with the event. Possible values include:
-Action Recommended: Action may be required by you to avoid possible disruptions or mitigate risks for your services. It is recommended to evaluate these actions and the potential impact on your services.
- False Positive: After investigation, we've determined your service is healthy and service issues did not impact your services as originally communicated.
- Preliminary PIR: For our largest, most impactful service issues a Preliminary Post Incident Review (PIR) is published generally within 72 hours of mitigation, to summarize what we have learned so far from the still-in-progress investigation.
- Final PIR: For service issues, a Final Post Incident Review (PIR) may be published to provide additional details or learnings. Sometimes this requires us to complete an internal retrospective, generally within 14 days of mitigation.

properties.name: recommendedActions
properties.doc: Recommended actions of event.

properties.name: message
properties.doc: Recommended action title for the service health event.

properties.name: actions
properties.doc: Recommended actions for the service health event.

valueType.name: ResourceHealthEventRecommendedActionsItem
valueType.doc: Recommended action for the service health event.

properties.name: groupId
properties.doc: Recommended action group Id for the service health event.

properties.name: actionText
properties.doc: Recommended action text

properties.name: localeCode
properties.doc: Recommended action locale for the service health event.

properties.name: faqs
properties.doc: Frequently asked questions for the service health event.

valueType.name: ResourceHealthEventFaq
valueType.doc: Frequently asked question for the service health event

properties.name: question
properties.doc: FAQ question for the service health event.

properties.name: answer
properties.doc: FAQ answer for the service health event.

properties.name: localeCode
properties.doc: FAQ locale for the service health event.

properties.name: IsHirEvent
properties.doc: It provides information if the event is High incident rate event or not.

properties.name: IsMicrosoftSupportEnabled
properties.doc: Tells if we want to enable or disable Microsoft Support for this event.

properties.name: description
properties.doc: Contains the communication message for the event, that could include summary, root cause and other details. Use fetchEventDetails endpoint to get description of sensitive events.

properties.name: IsPlatformInitiated
properties.doc: Is true if the event is platform initiated.

properties.name: IsChatWithUsEnabled
properties.doc: Tells if we want to enable or disable Microsoft Support for this event.

properties.name: priority
properties.doc: Priority level of the event. Has value from 0 to 23. 0 is the highest priority. Service issue events have higher priority followed by planned maintenance and health advisory. Critical events have higher priority followed by error, warning and informational. Furthermore, active events have higher priority than resolved.

properties.name: lastUpdateTime
properties.doc: It provides the Timestamp for when the health impacting event was last updated.

properties.name: hirStage
properties.doc: Stage for HIR Document

properties.name: additionalInformation
properties.doc: Additional information

properties.name: message
properties.doc: Additional information Message

properties.name: duration
properties.doc: duration in seconds

properties.name: impactType
properties.doc: The type of the impact

properties.name: eventTags
properties.doc:
A list of metadata tags associated with the event. Possible values include:
-Action Recommended: Action may be required by you to avoid possible disruptions or mitigate risks for your services. It is recommended to evaluate these actions and the potential impact on your services.
- False Positive: After investigation, we've determined your service is healthy and service issues did not impact your services as originally communicated.
- Preliminary PIR: For our largest, most impactful service issues a Preliminary Post Incident Review (PIR) is published generally within 72 hours of mitigation, to summarize what we have learned so far from the still-in-progress investigation.
- Final PIR: For service issues, a Final Post Incident Review (PIR) may be published to provide additional details or learnings. Sometimes this requires us to complete an internal retrospective, generally within 14 days of mitigation.

description.value: Virtual Machines case

title.value: Automatic updates to the dial tone page from ACM - SHD event

description.value: All Azure SQL service management requests (create, update, delete, etc.) are serviced through the SQL Control Plane infrastructure. Engineers determined that during this issue, the control plane service became unhealthy after receiving a significant increase in internally generated operations and reaching an operational threshold. This led to service management requests becoming unable to complete, which in-turn resulted in timeouts and throttling. Subsequent investigation by engineers determined this issue was due service requests from an internal Azure group that triggered a bug which caused an excessive number of internally generated operations.

title.value: Azure SQL Database - West Europe
Automatic updates to the dial tone page from ACM - banner

message.value: A subset of customers using Storage in West Europe experienced service availability issues. In addition, resources with dependencies on the impacted storage scale units may have experienced downstream impact in the form of availability issues, connection failures, or high latency. Engineers are investigating the root cause.

title.value: Storage - West Europe
